name: Vietnam

colors.symbolism: revolution and the blood of those who died in the struggle for national liberation
the spirit of the Vietnamese people; gold is also the traditional Vietnamese national colour

description: A red field with a large centred five-pointed yellow star.
history: Designed by Nguyen Huu Tien in 1940 for the Cochinchina uprising and adopted as the flag of the Viet Minh, then as the national flag of the Democratic Republic of Vietnam (North Vietnam) on 30 November 1955 (proportions of the star were slightly modified from the 1945 original). It became the flag of the reunified Socialist Republic of Vietnam on 2 July 1976, following the end of the Vietnam War and the reunification of North and South.

features.symbolism: the colour of revolution and the blood shed in the struggle for independence

the leadership of the Communist Party of Vietnam; the five points have also been interpreted as representing the five classes that participated in the revolution — workers, peasants, soldiers, intellectuals, and youth
